home.title: Defqon Companion
home.subtitle: Your guide to the hardest festival on Earth

colors.title: Colors of Defqon.1
colors.subtitle: Each color represents a unique stage and music style

red.subtitle: Main stage — the heart of Defqon.1
red.short: The legendary main stage where the biggest names in hardstyle perform.
red.long: RED is where the magic happens. The main stage hosts the most iconic hardstyle artists, delivering euphoric melodies and powerful kicks to tens of thousands of fans. This is where the opening ceremonies, anthem reveals, and legendary closing shows take place.

blue.subtitle: Raw & mainstream raw hardstyle
blue.short: Harder kicks, darker atmosphere, raw energy.
blue.long: BLUE brings the raw power. Expect aggressive kicks, dark melodies, and an intense atmosphere. This stage is for those who prefer their hardstyle with more edge and grit, featuring the hardest-hitting raw artists.

black.subtitle: Pure hardcore
black.short: Faster, harder, louder. The home of hardcore.
black.long: BLACK is the domain of hardcore — faster BPMs, distorted kicks, and relentless energy. This stage pushes the limits of intensity and is beloved by the most dedicated hardcore fans.

yellow.subtitle: Frenchcore, uptempo & terror
yellow.short: Ultra-fast and distorted. Born in France, loved worldwide.
yellow.long: YELLOW is where speed meets chaos. Frenchcore, uptempo, and terror genres dominate this stage with BPMs pushing well above 190. If you want non-stop energy and distorted madness, this is your color.

indigo.subtitle: The darkest corner of raw hardstyle
indigo.short: Raw pushed to the extreme. Industrial and uncompromising.
indigo.long: INDIGO goes beyond standard raw hardstyle into extra raw territory. Think industrial sounds, brutal kicks, and an atmosphere that feels underground and unapologetic. This stage is for the purists of hard sound.

magenta.subtitle: The golden era of hardstyle
magenta.short: Nostalgia meets energy. The classics that started it all.
magenta.long: MAGENTA is a tribute to the roots of hardstyle. Reverse bass kicks, iconic melodies from the 2000s, and the tracks that built the scene. This stage is pure nostalgia for veterans and a history lesson for newcomers.

silver.subtitle: Mechanical, metallic, industrial
silver.short: Where hardcore meets industrial. Cold, hard, metallic.
silver.long: SILVER channels an industrial aesthetic — think mechanical rhythms, metallic textures, and hardcore beats that feel like they belong in a dystopian factory. A niche but fiercely loyal audience.

gold.subtitle: Old-school rave & millennium hardcore
gold.short: The roots of the rave scene. 90s and early 2000s vibes.
gold.long: GOLD celebrates the origins of rave culture and millennium hardcore. Expect classic rave anthems, old-school gabber, and the sounds that defined electronic music in the 90s and early 2000s.

purple.subtitle: New artists & rising stars
purple.short: Where tomorrow's legends are born. Fresh talent showcase.
purple.long: PURPLE is the launchpad for emerging talent. This stage showcases the next generation of hardstyle, hardcore, and hard dance artists. Many of today's biggest names once started on this very stage.

guide.title: Discover Hardstyle
guide.subtitle: Everything you need to know before your first Defqon.1

history.title: History of Defqon.1
history.originText: The name Defqon.1 comes from DEFCON, the American military alert level system. It's a stylization of DEFCON 1, which represents the maximum alert level. The idea behind this name is to convey an image of power, intensity and maximum tension, in line with the very hard and energetic universe of the festival.
history.whyOneText: The number 1 refers directly to the highest level of the DEFCON system. It's not an arbitrary number: it symbolizes the maximum level of vigilance. In the festival context, this "1" reinforces the idea of an extreme event, with very strong energy, designed as an intense experience from start to finish.
history.colorsText: Defqon.1 has built a visual universe based on the "Colors of Defqon.1". Each color corresponds to a musical style, a stage, or a particular atmosphere of the festival. Each color helps the public navigate the festival while reinforcing the feeling of belonging to a coherent and immersive musical world.
history.brandText: Defqon.1 is not just a name: it's a highly structured brand universe. The festival combines a strong name, a striking military reference, and a precise color code to create an immediately recognizable identity.

hardstyle.title: What is Hardstyle?
hardstyle.text: Hardstyle is a genre of electronic dance music born in the early 2000s in the Netherlands. It's characterized by its hard-hitting kick drums, euphoric melodies, and intense energy. The genre typically ranges from 150 to 160 BPM.
hardstyle.euphoric: Euphoric Hardstyle (RED) — Melodic, uplifting, emotional. The most accessible subgenre with beautiful melodies over powerful kicks. 150–160 BPM.
hardstyle.raw: Raw Hardstyle (BLUE) — Darker, more aggressive kicks and screeches. Less melody, more raw energy and industrial sounds. 150–160 BPM.
hardstyle.extraRaw: Extra Raw (INDIGO) — Raw hardstyle pushed to the extreme. Industrial textures, brutal kicks, underground atmosphere. 150–165 BPM.
hardstyle.hardcore: Hardcore (BLACK) — Faster, harder, more extreme. Distorted kicks at 160–200+ BPM. A genre of its own with deep roots in the Netherlands.
hardstyle.frenchcore: Frenchcore (YELLOW) — Born in France, 190–210 BPM, distorted kicks and intense drops. Pure energy on the dance floor.
hardstyle.uptempo: Uptempo (YELLOW) — The bridge between hardcore and frenchcore. 180–220 BPM, relentless tempo with bouncy kicks.
hardstyle.terror: Terror (YELLOW) — The most extreme tempo: 200–300+ BPM. Noisy, chaotic, not for the faint of heart.
hardstyle.industrial: Industrial Hardcore (SILVER) — Where hardcore meets industrial music. Mechanical rhythms, metallic textures, dystopian vibes. 170–200 BPM.
hardstyle.classic: Classic/Early Hardstyle (MAGENTA) — The original sound from 2000–2008. Reverse bass, simpler melodies, nostalgic vibes. 140–155 BPM.
hardstyle.earlyRave: Early Rave / Gabber (GOLD) — The roots of the rave scene. 90s and early 2000s anthems, old-school gabber, millennium hardcore. 150–180 BPM.
hardstyle.hardTrance: Hard Trance (ORANGE) — Where trance meets hard dance. Driving basslines, euphoric builds and acid synths. The roots of many hardstyle artists.
hardstyle.hardTechno: Hard Techno (GREEN) — The current crossover wave. Industrial techno meets hard dance energy. Some hardstyle artists' alter egos perform here.
hardstyle.dnb: Drum & Bass (PINK) — Fast breakbeats at 170–180 BPM. Introduced at Defqon.1 in 2025 with its own dedicated stage.
hardstyle.happyHardcore: Happy Hardcore (UV) — Fast, joyful, and uplifting. 160–180 BPM with pitched-up vocals and euphoric piano melodies. Think Darren Styles and Da Tweekaz crossovers.

practical.title: Practical Info
practical.gettingThereText: Defqon.1 takes place at Walibi Holland, Biddinghuizen, Netherlands. Shuttle buses run from Amsterdam, Rotterdam, and Utrecht. Car parking is available on-site — arrive early to secure a good spot. There is no public transport directly to the venue.
practical.campingText: Camping opens on Thursday morning. Bring all your own gear — tents, sleeping bags, chairs, etc. No glass is allowed on the campsite. Lockers are available for rent. Showers and toilets are provided on-site.
practical.whatToBringText: Earplugs (essential!), sunscreen, rain poncho, comfortable shoes, power bank, cash + card, ID/passport, reusable water bottle. These basics will make your festival experience much more enjoyable.
practical.rulesText: 18+ only — ID is checked at the entrance. No drugs. No professional cameras. No glass bottles. No weapons or sharp objects. Make sure to read the full terms on the official Defqon.1 website before attending.
practical.tipsText: Arrive on Thursday for The Gathering — it's the perfect warm-up. Explore all stages, not just your favorite. Eat before heading to the stages. Stay hydrated throughout the day. Download the Q-dance app for the official timetable. Meet at the Defqon.1 flag for meetups with friends.
practical.weatherText: Late June in the Netherlands — expect anything. Hot sun during the day, cold at night. Pack layers, and always have a rain poncho ready. Sunscreen during the day, a hoodie for the evening.

vocabulary.title: Vocabulary
vocabulary.kick: Kick — The heavy bass drum hit that defines hardstyle. The most important element of any track.
vocabulary.reverseBass: Reverse Bass — A kick technique where the bass tail plays before the punch. Signature of early hardstyle.
vocabulary.breakdown: Breakdown — The melodic mid-section of a track, building tension before the climax.
vocabulary.climax: Climax — The peak moment when melody and kicks combine at full power. The moment everyone waits for.
vocabulary.screeches: Screeches — High-pitched, distorted synthesizer sounds typical of raw hardstyle.
vocabulary.antiClimactic: Anti-climax — When the build-up leads to a raw/unexpected drop instead of a melody. A raw hardstyle signature move.
vocabulary.bpm: BPM — Beats Per Minute. Hardstyle is 150–160, hardcore 160–200+, frenchcore 190–210, terror can go 300+.
vocabulary.gabber: Gabber — Dutch hardcore movement from the 90s. Fast, aggressive, distorted. The ancestor of today's hardcore scene.
vocabulary.shuffle: Melbourne Shuffle — The iconic dance style associated with hardstyle. Quick footwork, heel-toe sliding movements.
vocabulary.anthem: Anthem — The official track of a festival edition, revealed at the event. Being chosen as anthem creator is the highest honor.
vocabulary.endshow: Endshow — The spectacular closing performance of a stage, featuring fireworks, pyro, and a special DJ set.
vocabulary.powerHour: POWER HOUR — Defqon.1's legendary moment: one hour of back-to-back hits, fireworks, and the famous left-right crowd movement.
vocabulary.qdance: Q-dance — The Dutch event organizer behind Defqon.1, Qlimax, and many other hardstyle events. Founded in 1999.
vocabulary.hardBass: Hardbass — Not to be confused with hardstyle. A fast, Russian electronic style. Sometimes jokingly played at festivals.
vocabulary.set: Set — A DJ's performance. Typically 60 minutes at festivals. A B2B set means two DJs playing back-to-back.

music.title: Music
music.subtitle: Playlists to get you ready for Defqon.1

headhunterz.bio: One of the most iconic figures in hardstyle history. Rose to prominence in the late 2000s and became the genre's biggest crossover act. Temporarily left hardstyle (2014-2017) to explore EDM before making a celebrated return.
headhunterz.fact: His track "Dragonborn" is considered one of the greatest hardstyle tracks ever made. Highest-ranked hardstyle DJ in DJ Mag Top 100 multiple times.

wildstylez.bio: Known for emotionally rich productions, a staple of the hardstyle scene since the mid-2000s. Formed the legendary duo Project One with Headhunterz, producing timeless anthems.
wildstylez.fact: Project One's "The Art of Creation" album is considered a milestone in hardstyle history.

dbstf.bio: Active since 2004, one of the longest-running and most respected duos in hardstyle. Celebrated for exceptional musicality, cinematic productions, and pioneering full-scale narrative live shows.
dbstf.fact: Created the Defqon.1 2024 anthem and multiple other major festival anthems.
dbstf.ghostStories: Ghost Stories is a cinematic live show concept featuring D-Block & S-te-Fan alongside vocalist Evelyn Bakker — Diederik's sister, who started as a dancer before lending her voice to the project. The show features an original storyline, custom visuals, and exclusive tracks for each edition. Three chapters: Ghost Stories 1 (2022, AFAS Live Amsterdam), Ghost Stories 2 (2023), and Ghost Stories 3 (2024), each with its own narrative arc and soundtrack album. The final show took place in November 2025 for DBSTF's 20th anniversary. Widely considered one of the greatest hardstyle show concepts ever — it elevated the genre into a full narrative experience with film-quality storytelling.

sound-rush.bio: Quickly rose through the ranks with high-energy euphoric productions. Signed to Dirty Workz, they became one of the most consistent hitmakers in modern euphoric hardstyle.

brennan-heart.bio: A veteran producer active since the early 2000s, one of hardstyle's most prolific anthem creators. Runs the I AM Hardstyle label and event brand.
brennan-heart.fact: "Imaginary" is one of the most-played hardstyle tracks of all time. Created the Defqon.1 2016 anthem.

b-front.bio: Occupies a unique space between raw and melodic hardstyle, crafting dark, atmospheric, and emotionally intense tracks. Revered for his uncompromising artistic vision.
b-front.fact: Often cited by fans and peers as the most "authentic" artist in hardstyle — a true artist's artist. His Qlimax sets are legendary.

warface.bio: One of the most energetic raw hardstyle acts, known for aggressive kicks, dark atmospheres, and intense live performances. A driving force in pushing raw hardstyle forward.
warface.fact: "I Need You" became a massive crossover hit within the scene. Runs the Art of Dance events.

radical-redemption.bio: Self-styled as the "One Man Army," one of the biggest names in raw hardstyle with a massive catalog. Hosts his own sold-out solo arena events.
radical-redemption.fact: His solo show "The One Man Army" at AFAS Live sells out consistently — one of the few raw hardstyle acts to headline solo arena events.

rebelion.bio: Known for ultra-hard, industrial-leaning raw hardstyle with punishing kicks and dark themes. Pushed the boundaries of how aggressive raw hardstyle could get while maintaining musicality.
rebelion.fact: Their "Overdose" album defined a new wave of extreme raw hardstyle.

sub-zero-project.bio: Bridge the gap between euphoric and raw hardstyle, combining hard kicks with big melodies. Rose meteorically through the ranks in the late 2010s.
sub-zero-project.fact: Known for their "PSYchedelic" exploration blending psy-trance elements into hardstyle. Created the Defqon.1 2023 closing ceremony anthem.

angerfist.bio: The undisputed king of mainstream hardcore, active since the early 2000s. His masked persona and relentless production output have made him the most recognized hardcore act worldwide.
angerfist.fact: #1 in DJ Mag Hardcore Top 100 for multiple consecutive years. His Defqon.1 and Masters of Hardcore sets are considered definitive hardcore experiences.

miss-k8.bio: The most prominent female artist in hardcore, known for fierce mixing skills and intense track selection. Originally from Ukraine, she became a global touring act.
miss-k8.fact: First woman to win the DJ Mag Hardcore Top 100, ranking #1. Has headlined virtually every major hardcore event globally.

nosferatu.bio: A hardcore veteran active since the 1990s, part of the old guard that helped shape the genre. Known for his dark, cinematic style and extraordinary longevity.
nosferatu.fact: Over 25 years of producing and performing, making him one of the longest-active hardcore artists. His dark atmospheric style influenced many newer producers.

sefa.bio: Broke through as a teenager, becoming the youngest artist to play a mainstage at Defqon.1. Widely credited with bringing frenchcore into the mainstream hardstyle festival circuit.
sefa.fact: Played the Defqon.1 mainstage at just 19 years old. Founded his own label Generation HRD.

dr-peacock.bio: One of the key figures who popularized frenchcore internationally. Founded Peacock Records, the premier frenchcore label, and mentored several artists including Sefa.
dr-peacock.fact: "Trip to Ireland" became one of the most recognized frenchcore anthems, blending Irish folk melodies with hard kicks.

showtek.bio: Among the biggest hardstyle acts in the mid-2000s before making a high-profile — and controversial — genre switch to mainstream EDM around 2012. Achieved massive global commercial success.
showtek.fact: "FTS" remains a beloved hardstyle anthem. Their EDM track "Booyah" and collab with David Guetta "Bad" reached hundreds of millions of streams.

the-prophet.bio: Considered one of the founding fathers of hardstyle, active since the late 1990s when the genre was still emerging from gabber and hard trance. Co-founded Scantraxx Records.
the-prophet.fact: Scantraxx Records (est. 2002) launched the careers of Headhunterz, Wildstylez, and many other top acts.

vertile.bio: A new generation raw hardstyle producer who has risen rapidly with a powerful, polished sound. Represents the modern wave of hardstyle production with crisp sound design.
vertile.fact: Created the Defqon.1 2025 anthem — a career-defining milestone for any hardstyle artist.

d-sturb.bio: Established himself as one of the top raw hardstyle producers with consistent high-quality releases. His sound bridges raw power with melodic elements, earning wide appeal.
d-sturb.fact: Selected to produce the Defqon.1 2026 anthem "Sacred Oath," cementing his status as one of the genre's leading figures.

phuture-noize.bio: Known for his deeply artistic and conceptual approach to hardstyle, releasing full album projects with overarching narratives and themes. His productions are considered among the most musically sophisticated in the genre.
phuture-noize.fact: His album "Silver Bullet" (2019) is widely regarded as one of the greatest hardstyle albums ever made.

ran-d.bio: Achieved something extremely rare in hardstyle: a genuine crossover mainstream hit. A veteran producer with decades of experience, respected for both harder and melodic productions.
ran-d.fact: "Zombie" (a hardstyle cover of The Cranberries) went viral in 2019 with hundreds of millions of streams — one of the biggest hardstyle crossover moments ever.

coone.bio: Belgium's most prominent hardstyle export and a key ambassador for the genre. Active since the mid-2000s, running his own label Dirty Workz.
coone.fact: Founded Dirty Workz, one of the most successful hardstyle labels. First Belgian artist to create a Defqon.1 anthem ("Survival of the Fittest," 2014).

da-tweekaz.bio: Norwegian duo known for lighthearted, crowd-pleasing hardstyle with humor, pop culture references, and sing-along melodies. One of the few successful Scandinavian hardstyle acts.
da-tweekaz.fact: Famous for their unofficial Disney hardstyle bootlegs (Frozen's "Let It Go," The Lion King's "Circle of Life") which became massive crowd favorites.

atmozfears.bio: Known for cinematic, emotionally rich productions that emphasize atmosphere and melody. Characterized by lush pads, sweeping builds, and polished production.
atmozfears.fact: Created the Qlimax 2018 anthem "Keep Me Awake." Also known for sharing in-depth production tutorials with the community.

frontliner.bio: Widely regarded as one of the most technically gifted producers in hardstyle history. Known for pushing production boundaries and innovating with sound design.
frontliner.fact: "Halos" is considered a genre-defining classic. Often called the "producer's producer" for his mastery of synthesizer programming and kick design.
